name: Majma ibn Abdullah
title: Narrator of A'idh
shortDescription: A companion of the Imam who delivered the news of Kufa at Udhayb al-Hijanat along with Tirimmah and was martyred in Karbala.
fullStory:
Majma' ibn Abdullah al-A'idhi was a brave and well-known Shia. He, along with a few others (including Tirimmah ibn Uday), reached Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan at the station of 'Udhayb al-Hijanat'.

Hurr ibn Yazid al-Riyahi, who was tasked with preventing anyone from joining the Imam, intended to arrest or turn them back, but Imam Hussain (AS) said: 'These are my companions, and I will protect them.' Right there, Majma' informed the Imam of the bitter news of Kufa and the martyrdom of Qays ibn Mus-hir.

Fully aware of the harsh conditions ahead, Majma' stayed with the Imam and stepped into Karbala. On Ashura, he bravely lined up against Ibn Sa'd's army.

Finally, after a fierce battle alongside the other companions, he attained martyrdom defending the sanctuary of the Ahl al-Bayt (AS), and his name was immortalized in the Ziyarat al-Nahiya al-Muqaddasa.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى مَجْمَعِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ العائِذِيِّ...
quote: (Ziyarat al-Nahiya:) Peace be upon Majma' ibn Abdullah al-A'idhi...
